Technology.Name: Transformer
Technology.Description: Attention-based neural architecture used by most modern LLMs.

Technology.Name: Mixture of Experts (MoE)
Technology.Description: Scales model capacity by routing tokens to specialized expert networks.

Technology.Name: RNN Derivatives
Technology.Description: Legacy architectures (LSTM/GRU) used before transformers; sometimes combined with attention.

Technology.Name: Supervised Pretraining
Technology.Description: Training on large text corpora to predict next tokens.

Technology.Name: Instruction Tuning
Technology.Description: Fine-tuning with curated instruction–response datasets.

Technology.Name: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)
Technology.Description: Alignment technique using reward models and policy optimization.

Technology.Name: DPO (Direct Preference Optimization)
Technology.Description: Alternative to RLHF that directly optimizes preference loss without RL loops.

Technology.Name: Continual / Online Learning
Technology.Description: Updates model over time without full retraining.

Technology.Name: Quantization
Technology.Description: Reduces weight precision (e.g., 8-bit, 4-bit) to speed up inference.

Technology.Name: Pruning
Technology.Description: Removes redundant parameters to reduce model size.

Technology.Name: Distillation
Technology.Description: Trains smaller models to emulate larger teacher LLMs.

Technology.Name: Speculative Decoding
Technology.Description: Accelerates generation using draft and verifier models.

Technology.Name: KV Caching
Technology.Description: Stores attention key/values to avoid recomputation in autoregressive decoding.

Technology.Name: Batching / Continuous Batching
Technology.Description: Combines multiple requests for higher throughput.

Technology.Name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
Technology.Description: Integrates external document retrieval into LLM responses.

Technology.Name: Long-Context Attention Mechanisms
Technology.Description: Techniques like sliding window, recurrence, or ALiBi enabling >100K context tokens.

Technology.Name: Memory Systems
Technology.Description: Persistent or session memory extending the model’s ability to reference past information.

Technology.Name: Vector Databases
Technology.Description: Stores and retrieves high-dimensional embeddings efficiently.

Technology.Name: Embedding Models
Technology.Description: Generate dense vector representations for semantic similarity.

Technology.Name: Tool Use / Function Calling
Technology.Description: Structured invocation of tools or APIs based on model output.

Technology.Name: Agent Frameworks
Technology.Description: Systems coordinating LLM tasks with memory, tools, and planning.

Technology.Name: Inference Runtimes
Technology.Description: Optimized execution engines for LLMs.

Technology.Name: Containerization
Technology.Description: Packaging and distribution using Docker, OCI images, and orchestration platforms.

Technology.Name: Distributed Training Frameworks
Technology.Description: Scaling LLM training across multiple GPUs/nodes.
